steps.title: Rearrange to expose the no-solution condition
steps.content:
For a linear equation to have no solution, all variable terms must cancel when rearranged, leaving a contradiction. Starting with $5x + 3 = rx + 7$, collect the $x$ terms on one side:
$$5x - rx = 7 - 3$$
$$(5 - r)x = 4$$
For this equation to have no solution, the coefficient of $x$ must be zero (so we get $0 \cdot x = 4$, or $0 = 4$, which is false). Therefore:
$$5 - r = 0$$
$$r = 5$$
That matches choice D — D is correct.

steps.title: Verify the no-solution condition
steps.content:
Substituting $r = 5$ back into the original equation confirms:
$$5x + 3 = 5x + 7$$
$$3 = 7$$
This is a contradiction (3 never equals 7), so there is no value of $x$ that satisfies the equation, confirming our answer.
